remember: Students will be able to recall the major events and dates related to the paths to modernisation in India.
Students will be able to identify and describe the key institutions and policies introduced during the colonial period.
Students will be able to list the major social and economic changes that took place during this period.
understand: Students will be able to explain the role of the British East India Company in the modernisation of India.
Students will be able to describe the impact of colonial policies on Indian society and economy.
Students will be able to understand the concept of 'Orientalism' and its relevance to Indian history.
apply: Students will be able to apply the knowledge of colonial policies to understand the present-day social and economic issues in India.
Students will be able to use historical data to compare the economic growth of India during the colonial period with other countries.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of colonialism on Indian culture and identity.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the relationship between colonialism and the development of Indian nationalism.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of key individuals and groups in shaping the modernisation process in India.
Students will be able to identify and explain the strengths and weaknesses of different interpretations of Indian modernisation.

ncert_objectives: Explain the concept of Orientalism and its impact on Indian society and culture.
Describe the key features of the colonial economy in India and its impact on Indian society.
Analyze the role of the Indian National Congress in the modernisation process in India.
board_exam_objectives: Describe the major events and policies introduced by the British colonial government in India.
Explain the impact of colonialism on the Indian economy and society.
Compare and contrast the modernisation process in India with other colonies.
